'''Manase Manuokafoa''' (born 24 March 1985) is a former [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tongan]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|pr}} for [[RC Albi XIII]] in the [[Elite One Championship]].

==Background==
He was born in [[Sydney, New South Wales]], Australia.

==Playing career==
As a [[Mascot Juniors|Mascot Jets]] junior who has come through every South Sydney representative junior grades, in 2005 he came through to the [[NRL]] in the green and red of the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], scoring two tries in his first season.

The following year, Manuokafoa played 19 games as the club finished last on the table.  In the [[2007 NRL season]], he was part of the South Sydney team which reached the finals for the first time since 1989.  Manuokafoa played in the club's elimination final loss against [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly-Warringah]] at [[Brookvale Oval]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2014-10-01/rugby-league27s-greatest-drought-breakers/5781544/|title=NRL Grand Final: South Sydney Rabbitohs looking to join rugby league's greatest drought breakers|newspaper=ABC News|date=October 2014 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/other-sports/list/the-worst-teams-in-nrl-history-newcastle-knights-rugby-league-wooden-spoon-parramatta-eels-salary-cap-semi-radradra-kieran-foran/1k2c6o18od8431jen234fqd91u/4/|title=The worst teams in NRL history|website=Sporting News|date=28 June 2016|access-date=6 January 2020|archive-date=11 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191211141048/https://www.sportingnews.com/au/other-sports/list/the-worst-teams-in-nrl-history-newcastle-knights-rugby-league-wooden-spoon-parramatta-eels-salary-cap-semi-radradra-kieran-foran/1k2c6o18od8431jen234fqd91u/4/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

In July 2008, Manuokafoa signed a three-year deal with the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] from the [[2009 NRL season]].<ref name="2009 NRL Player Movements">{{cite web|url=http://www.nrllive.com.au/2009-nrl-player-movements/ |title=2009 NRL Player Movements |publisher=NRL Live |date=2008-10-04 |access-date=2008-10-04 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081005172545/http://www.nrllive.com.au/2009-nrl-player-movements/ |archive-date=5 October 2008 }}</ref>

Manuokafoa was named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup|2008 World Cup]] training squads for both [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] and [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] and ultimately went on to play for Tonga in the World Cup.<ref name="Tug-of-war over Mason">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_3935853,00.html|title=Tug-of-war over Mason|publisher=Sky Sports|date=2008-08-05|accessdate=2008-08-06}}</ref><ref name="Samoa, Tonga and Fiji name squads">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7658506.stm|title=Samoa, Tonga and Fiji name squads|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-08|access-date=2008-10-09}}</ref><ref name="Kiwis select Sonny Bill">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_3949034,00.html|title=Kiwis select Sonny Bill|publisher=Sky Sports|date=2008-08-08|accessdate=2008-08-08}}</ref>

He joined the [[Parramatta Eels]] in 2010 and made two appearances for the club before being released at the end of 2011.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.townsvillebulletin.com.au/article/2010/06/24/148771_cowboys.html |title=Tate signs with Cowboys | Townsville Bulletin Sport - Cowboys |accessdate=2012-09-19 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://archive.today/20120919055202/http://www.townsvillebulletin.com.au/article/2010/06/24/148771_cowboys.html |archivedate=19 September 2012 |df=dmy-all }}</ref>

He then moved to England to play in the [[Super League]] with the [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] side for the 2012 season.

With the Bradford club being relegated to the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] for the 2015 season, Manase signed a two-year deal with [[Super League]] side the [[Widnes Vikings]].

==Bradford Bulls==
'''2012''' - [[2012 Bradford Bulls season|2012 Season]]

Manuokafoa missed the entire pre-season games as a result of a rupture cruciate ligament before the friendly against [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]].

He missed Rounds 1-4 due to an injury. He featured in 23 consecutive games from Round 5 ([[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]) to Round 27 ([[Catalans Dragons]]). He also featured in the [[Challenge Cup]] against [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] and [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]. Manuokafoa scored against [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] (1 try) and [[London Broncos]] (1 try).

'''2013''' - [[2013 Bradford Bulls season|2013 Season]]

Manuokafoa signed a two-year extension to his contract in the off-season. He featured in the pre-season friendlies against [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]] and [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]. He scored against [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]] (1 try).

He featured in fourteen consecutive games from Round 1 ([[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]) to Round 14 ([[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]). He was injured for Rounds 15-18. Manuokafoa played in Round 19 ([[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]]) to Round 27 ([[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]]). He also featured in the [[Challenge Cup]] against [[Rochdale Hornets|Rochdale]]. He was injured for Round 5 of the [[Challenge Cup]].

'''2014''' - [[2014 Bradford Bulls season|2014 Season]]

Manuokafoa featured in the pre-season friendlies against [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]], [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]] and [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]].

Manase featured in Round 1 Castleford to Round 10 ([[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]) then in Round 14 ([[Catalans Dragons]]) to Round 27 ([[London Broncos]]). He also featured in Round 4 ([[Oldham R.L.F.C.]]) to the Quarter Final ([[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]) in the [[Challenge Cup]]. He scored against [[London Broncos]] (1 try).

==Widnes Vikings==

'''2015'''

Manuokafoa featured in Round 1 ([[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]) to Round 4 ([[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]]). Then in Round 6 ([[Hull Kingston Rovers]]) to Qualifier 7 ([[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]]). He played in the [[Challenge Cup]] in Round 6 ([[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]]) to the Quarter Final ([[St Helens R.F.C.]]).

'''2016'''

Manuokafoa featured in Round 1 ([[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]) to Round 9 ([[Catalans Dragons]]) then in Round 11 ([[Salford Red Devils|Salford]]) to Round 12 ([[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]). Manase played in the [[Challenge Cup]] in Round 5 ([[Rochdale Hornets|Rochdale]]).
